title: Protein structures
intro: A protein structure is a list of atoms with 3D coordinates — and once you load one, it's just a pandas DataFrame, so everything you already know applies. You'll load lysozyme from the PDB and measure it. Work through the notebook, then check in.
preview:
from biopandas.pdb import PandasPdb

atoms = PandasPdb().fetch_pdb("1AKI").df["ATOM"]
print(len(atoms[atoms["element_symbol"] == "C"]))   # carbon atoms
question: The check-in cell counts the carbon atoms in the 1AKI structure. How many are there?
